# @zakkster/lite-headless / backtop

"Back to top" floating button. Shows after the user scrolls past a
threshold; click scrolls back to top (smooth by default).

## Quick example

```js
import { createBackTop } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/backtop";

const bt = createBackTop({
    threshold: 200,        // px past which the button shows
    smooth: true,          // default true; set false for instant jump
    onActivate: (reason) => {},
});

bt.attachTarget(window);   // or an element for an internal scroll container
bt.attachButton(buttonEl);
```

## Options

| Option | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `threshold` | `200` | px past which the button shows |
| `smooth` | `true` | Smooth scroll vs instant jump |
| `onActivate` | - | `(reason: string) => void` |

## API

```ts
{
    isVisible(): boolean
    threshold(): number
    smooth: boolean       // readonly

    attachTarget(el: Element | Window | null): off
    attachButton(buttonEl): off
    scrollToTop(reason?: string): void

    destroy(): void
}
```

## Painted attributes

### Button (`[data-backtop]`)

- `data-visible` — boolean, true when scrolled past threshold
- `hidden` — when not visible (for SR + non-CSS)
- `aria-label="Back to top"` — only if not pre-set

## Scroll-target semantics

`attachTarget(window)` (default) listens to window scroll and reads
`window.scrollY`. Pass an `HTMLElement` to track that container's
`scrollTop` instead. `null` is equivalent to window.

Scroll listener uses `{ passive: true }` and throttles paint via
`requestAnimationFrame` — never reads layout during the scroll
event itself, so it's safe to attach on long pages.

## Wrapper (`<lite-backtop>`)

```html
<lite-backtop threshold="300">
    <button data-backtop-button class="fab" type="button">↑</button>
</lite-backtop>

<!-- Or scoped to a scroll container -->
<div id="article" style="overflow:auto; height: 600px;">
    <article>...</article>
    <lite-backtop target="#article" threshold="200">
        <button data-backtop-button>↑</button>
    </lite-backtop>
</div>
```

Attributes:
- `threshold` — px (default 200)
- `smooth` — set to `"false"` for instant jump
- `target` — CSS selector for the scroll container; defaults to window

Host methods: `scrollToTop(reason)`.
Host accessors: `isVisible`, `threshold`, `_backtopInstance`.

Event: `backtop` — `{ detail: { reason } }`.

## CSS contract

The button starts with `hidden`; consumer styles a `data-visible`
transition (opacity, transform) for the fade-in. Typical FAB pattern:

```css
[data-backtop] {
    position: fixed;
    bottom: 24px; right: 24px;
    width: 44px; height: 44px;
    border-radius: 50%;
    background: var(--surface);
    border: 1px solid var(--line);
    cursor: pointer;
    opacity: 0; transform: translateY(8px);
    transition: opacity 180ms, transform 180ms;
}
[data-backtop][data-visible] { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0); }
[data-backtop][hidden] { display: none; }   /* override inline-flex */
```
